source.title: The Psychology and Neuroscience of Curiosity

confidence_rationale: Major Neuron Perspective synthesising the curiosity / information-seeking literature across psychology + neuroscience. Argues that 'curiosity' is best understood not as a unitary mechanism but as a family of motivations for information-seeking with overlapping but distinguishable behavioural and neural signatures. Provides the framing that R1 should be answered by treating curiosity as a structured channel that operates ALONGSIDE noise, not as a replacement.
failure_signatures: Treating 'curiosity' as a single substrate with a single threshold (the temptation in REE's MECH-216 schema-readout-wanting framing) will fail to capture the heterogeneity Kidd & Hayden document; the substrate may need multiple sub-mechanisms for different curiosity flavours (perceptual / epistemic / diversive).

An REE substrate that conflates information-seeking with noise will fail to reproduce ethological-context-dependence of curiosity that Kidd & Hayden emphasise (curiosity is not a temperature parameter -- it is goal-shaped).

mapping.source_claim_statement: Curiosity is a family of information-seeking motivations rather than a single mechanism; it has neural correlates in OFC, ACC, and dopaminergic midbrain regions; it is influenced by uncertainty, novelty, and learning-progress; it should be studied in its ethological context rather than as an abstract psychological construct.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-065 should treat curiosity (MECH-314 candidate) as a STRUCTURED channel distinct from noise (MECH-313), with at least three sub-flavours that may need separate sub-MECHs: novelty-driven (Wittmann 2008 striatum), uncertainty-driven (Daw 2006 frontopolar), and learning-progress-driven (Schmidhuber / Pathak computational tradition). REE's existing MECH-216 schema-readout wanting partially covers the novelty-driven flavour. The other two are not yet substrate-landed.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Kidd & Hayden explicitly resist a single computational definition of curiosity, which makes the paper less directly actionable for substrate registration than Wilson 2014 (which gives a clean two-channel decomposition). Used here primarily as the field-survey anchor for the heterogeneity argument.
mapping.source_context: Neuron Perspective (review/opinion); Kidd cognitive psychology, Hayden monkey neurophysiology; major synthesis paper for the curiosity-as-research-program literature.

confidence_components.notes: Neuron Perspective; respected synthesis. Mapping fidelity moderate because the paper resists single computational framings -- it argues for ethological-context study, which is a stance more than a mechanism.
